items.title: Pool Partners With First Internet Bank and Visa to Offer Multi-User Consumer Financial Accounts
items.description: SAN FRANCISCO, October 06, 2025--Pool United, Inc., a San-Francisco-based consumer payments company, is launching a new multi-user financial account in partnership with First Internet Bank, Member FDIC and Visa®. This account, called a Pool, allows consumers to add multiple users to one shared account, assign roles and permissions, and spend directly from a shared balance using the Pool Visa® Debit Card.

items.title: Klarna Stock Gets First Ratings After IPO. A Lot of Analysts Say It’s a Buy.
items.description: The bank began coverage with a Buy rating and a target of $55 for the stock price, saying Klarna is “the market leader” in the buy-now, pay-later business.  Klarna has “a particularly strong franchise in Europe, where we believe Klarna is a new, emerging closed loop payment scheme, similar to  American Express  in the United States,” the analysts wrote.  The nod to AmEx, one of the largest credit-card providers in the U.S., parallels commentary from members of management, who told Barron’s ahead of the IPO that the company was aiming to challenge the traditional banking sector.

items.title: Can Predictive Intelligence Future-Proof Mastercard's Business Model?
items.description: MA is turning transaction data into predictive intelligence, using AI to power fraud detection and smarter commerce.

items.title: How Can Visa Modernize Liquidity Management With Stablecoins?
items.description: V is betting on stablecoins to modernize liquidity management, and it can change how businesses move money across borders.
